sn46.14:1.1: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha in the Bamboo Forest, the Squirrels’ Sanctuary.
sn46.14:1.2: And on that occasion Ven. Mahā Kassapa was staying in the Pepper Tree Cave: diseased, in pain, severely ill.
sn46.14:1.3: Then the Blessed One, emerging from his seclusion in the evening, went to Ven. Mahā Kassapa and, on arrival, sat down on a seat made ready. Having sat down, he said to Ven. Mahā Kassapa,
sn46.14:2.1: “I hope you are getting better, Kassapa. I hope you are comfortable. I hope that your pains are lessening and not increasing. I hope that there are signs of their lessening, and not of their increasing.”
sn46.14:2.2: “I am not getting better, lord. I am not comfortable. My extreme pains are increasing, not lessening. There are signs of their increasing, and not of their lessening.”
sn46.14:3.1: “Kassapa, these seven factors for awakening rightly taught by me, when developed and pursued, lead to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding.
sn46.14:3.3: “Mindfulness as a factor for awakening rightly taught by me, when developed and pursued, leads to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding. “Analysis of qualities as a factor for awakening, rightly taught by me, when developed and pursued, leads to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding. “Persistence as a factor for awakening.… “Rapture as a factor for awakening.… “Calm as a factor for awakening.… “Concentration as a factor for awakening.… “Equanimity as a factor for awakening rightly taught by me, when developed and pursued, leads to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding.
sn46.14:3.4: “Kassapa, these are the seven factors for awakening rightly taught by me that—when developed and pursued—lead to direct knowledge, to self-awakening, to unbinding.”
sn46.14:3.5: “They are indeed factors for awakening, O Blessed One.
sn46.14:3.6: They are indeed factors for awakening, O One Well-Gone.”
sn46.14:4.1: That is what the Blessed One said.
sn46.14:4.2: Gratified, Ven. Mahā Kassapa delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
sn46.14:4.3: And Ven. Mahā Kassapa recovered from his disease.
sn46.14:4.4: That was how Ven. Mahā Kassapa’s disease was abandoned.
